[The words must be considered as meaningful English words and must not be related to each other based on the number of letters/number of consonants/vowels in the word] Calcium : Osteomalacia
questions.explanation: The relationship between Calcium and Osteomalacia is that a deficiency in calcium can lead to osteomalacia, a condition where bones become soft due to a lack of mineralization. Similarly, a deficiency in Iron can lead to Anaemia, a condition where the blood lacks enough healthy red blood cells, often caused by insufficient iron.

questions.question: When did Mahatma Gandhi establish 'All India Harijan sevak Sangh'?
questions.explanation: Harijan Sevak Sangh was founded by Mahatma Gandhi in 1932 to eradicate untouchability in India, working for Harijan and upliftment of the depressed class of India.

questions.question: At which gate of the Red Fort did Jawaharlal Nehru hoist the Indian national flag on August 15, 1947?
questions.explanation: India's first Prime Minister Jawaharlal Nehru unfurled the national flag for the the first time over the Lohori Gate of the Red Fort on 15th August, 1947. > भारत के प्रथम प्रधानमंत्री जवाहरलाल नेहरू ने 15 अगस्त 1947 को लाल किले के लोहोरी गेट पर पहली बार राष्ट्रीय ध्वज फहराया था।

questions.question: The Suri king ____ was defeated by Humayun to regain his kingdom. > सूरी राजा ___ को हुमायूँ ने पराजित कर अपना राज्य पुनः प्राप्त कर लिया।
questions.explanation: Humayun ruled India for nearly a decade but was ousted by Sher Shah Suri, the Afghan ruler. Humayun wandered for about 15 years after his defeat. Meanwhile, Sher Shah Suri died and Humayun was able to defeat his successor, Sikandar Suri regain his crown of the Hindustan. However, soon after, he died in 1556 at a young age of 48 years. > हुमायूं ने लगभग एक दशक तक भारत पर शासन किया, लेकिन अफ़गान शासक शेर शाह सूरी ने उसे हटा दिया। अपनी हार के बाद हुमायूं लगभग 15 साल तक भटकता रहा। इस बीच, शेर शाह सूरी की मृत्यु हो गई और हुमायूं उसके उत्तराधिकारी सिकंदर सूरी को हराकर हिंदुस्तान का ताज फिर से हासिल करने में सफल रहा। हालाँकि, इसके तुरंत बाद, 1556 में 48 साल की छोटी उम्र में उसकी मृत्यु हो गई।

questions.question: Kalinga’s King Kharvela was associated with which of the following dynasty?
questions.explanation: Most part of the present state of Odisha was known as Kalinga. In the ancient times Kharvel was the ruler of the Mahameghavahana dynasty. It was ruled by Kalinga King Kharavel who was a patron of Jainism as in the Hathigumpha inscription. The name of Kharavel's dynasty is also Chedi, The inscription mentions Mahapadmananda the ruler of the Nanda dynasty has constructed a canal in Kalinga. > वर्तमान ओडिशा राज्य का अधिकांश भाग कलिंग के नाम से जाना जाता था। प्राचीन काल में खारवेल महामेघवाहन वंश के शासक थे। इस पर कलिंग राजा खारवेल का शासन था जो जैन धर्म के संरक्षक थे, जैसा कि हाथीगुम्फा शिलालेख में बताया गया है। खारवेल के वंश का नाम भी चेदि था, शिलालेख में उल्लेख है कि नंद वंश के शासक महापद्मनंद ने कलिंग में एक नहर का निर्माण कराया था।

questions.question: Pandit Bhimsen Joshi received the National Film Award for best male playback singer for his rendition in which of the following Hindi movies of 1985?
questions.explanation: Pt. Bhimsen Joshi received the National Film Award for Best Male Playback Singer in the film Ankahee. He was awarded Padma Shri in 1972, Padma Bhushan in 1985, Padma Vibhushan in 1999 and Bharat Ratna in 2008. > पंडित भीमसेन जोशी को फिल्म अनकही में सर्वश्रेष्ठ पार्श्वगायक का राष्ट्रीय फिल्म पुरस्कार मिला। उन्हें 1972 में पद्म श्री, 1985 में पद्म भूषण, 1999 में पद्म विभूषण और 2008 में भारत रत्न से सम्मानित किया गया।

questions.question: What does infant mortality mean?
options: The number of deaths per 1,000 live births of children under 1 year of age.>1 वर्ष से कम आयु के प्रति 1,000 जीवित जन्मों पर बच्चों की मृत्यु की संख्या।
The number of deaths per 1,000 live births of children under the age of 5 years.>5 वर्ष से कम आयु के प्रति 1,000 जीवित जन्मों पर बच्चों की मृत्यु की संख्या।
The number of deaths per 1,000 live births of children under the age of 6 months.>6 महीने से कम आयु के प्रति 1,000 जीवित जन्मों पर बच्चों की मृत्यु की संख्या।
The number of deaths per 1,000 live births of children under the age of 3 years.>3 वर्ष से कम आयु के प्रति 1,000 जीवित जन्मों पर बच्चों की मृत्यु की संख्या।
questions.explanation: Infant Mortality Rate is the number of deaths per 1,000 live births of children under age of one year. > शिशु मृत्यु दर एक वर्ष से कम आयु के प्रति 1,000 जीवित जन्मों पर होने वाली मृत्यु की संख्या है।

questions.question: Vishvamitri River flows through which of the following cities?
questions.explanation: The Vishvamitri River flows through the city of Vadodara in Gujarat, India. Specifically, it passes through the western part of Vadodara, dividing the city into two parts, before ultimately merging with the Gulf of Khambhat. > विश्वामित्री नदी भारत के गुजरात राज्य के वडोदरा शहर से होकर बहती है। खास तौर पर, यह वडोदरा के पश्चिमी हिस्से से होकर गुजरती है, शहर को दो भागों में विभाजित करती है, और अंततः खंभात की खाड़ी में मिल जाती है।

questions.question: Which of the following is the world's thirdhighest mountain that connects the borders of India and Nepal?
questions.explanation: Kanchenjunga is the world's third highest mountain with an elevation of 28, 169 feet [9396 metres]. Nepal has the highest mountain in the world, Mount Everest as well as 1310 peaks over 6000 m height. > कंचनजंगा दुनिया का तीसरा सबसे ऊँचा पर्वत है जिसकी ऊँचाई 28, 169 फीट [9396 मीटर] है। नेपाल में दुनिया का सबसे ऊँचा पर्वत माउंट एवरेस्ट है और साथ ही 6000 मीटर से ज़्यादा ऊँचाई वाली 1310 चोटियाँ हैं।

questions.question: Which of the following channels or straits is located between the Great Nicobar Island and Indonesia's Sumatra Island?
questions.explanation: The Grand Channel is also known as "Six Degree Channel" as because it lies 6º North to the equator. It lies in between Great Nicobar islands and Sumatra Island. It is suitable for navigation by large merchant ships. > ग्रैंड चैनल को "सिक्स डिग्री चैनल" के नाम से भी जाना जाता है क्योंकि यह भूमध्य रेखा से 6º उत्तर में स्थित है। यह ग्रेट निकोबार द्वीप और सुमात्रा द्वीप के बीच में स्थित है। यह बड़े व्यापारी जहाजों के लिए नौवहन के लिए उपयुक्त है।

questions.question: The passageway, which has been renamed as Atal Tunnel, passes through the _____pass. > यह मार्ग, जिसका नाम बदलकर अटल सुरंग रखा गया है, _____दर्रे से होकर गुजरता है।
questions.explanation: On 25th Dec 2019, on the birthday of former Atal Bihari Vajpayee, the name of Rohtang Tunnel was changed to Atal Tunnel. The tunnel passes through Rohtang Pass [Himachal Pradesh] connecting Manali and Leh. It has length of 9.02 km, on the elevation of 3000m. > 25 दिसंबर 2019 को पूर्व प्रधानमंत्री अटल बिहारी वाजपेयी के जन्मदिन पर रोहतांग सुरंग का नाम बदलकर अटल सुरंग कर दिया गया। यह सुरंग रोहतांग दर्रे [हिमाचल प्रदेश] से होकर गुजरती है और मनाली और लेह को जोड़ती है। इसकी लंबाई 9.02 किलोमीटर है और यह 3000 मीटर की ऊंचाई पर है।

questions.question: Neil Wagner, who recently retired from international cricket, is from which country?
questions.explanation: New Zealand fast bowler Neil Wagner announced his retirement from international cricket on February 27, 2024. > न्यूजीलैंड के तेज गेंदबाज नील वैगनर ने 27 फरवरी 2024 को अंतरराष्ट्रीय क्रिकेट से संन्यास की घोषणा की।

questions.question: Which state recently launched the ‘NTR Bharosa pension scheme’?
questions.explanation: Andhra Pradesh Chief Minister Chandrababu Naidu launched the NTR Bharosa pension scheme on 1 July 2024, delivering pensions personally to beneficiaries. > आंध्र प्रदेश के मुख्यमंत्री चंद्रबाबू नायडू ने 1 जुलाई 2024 को एनटीआर भरोसा पेंशन योजना शुरू की, जिसके तहत लाभार्थियों को व्यक्तिगत रूप से पेंशन प्रदान की जाएगी।

questions.question: Krystyna Pyszkova, who recently won Miss World 2024 title, belongs to which country?
questions.explanation: Krystyna Pyszkova of the Czech Republic won Miss World 2024 at the Jio World Cinema in Mumbai, succeeding Poland’s Karolina Bielawska. Alongside the main title, Pyszkova was honored with the Best Fashion Designer Award – Europe. > चेक गणराज्य की क्रिस्टीना पिस्ज़कोवा ने मुंबई के जियो वर्ल्ड सिनेमा में मिस वर्ल्ड 2024 का खिताब जीता, उन्होंने पोलैंड की कैरोलिना बिलावस्का को पीछे छोड़ा। मुख्य खिताब के साथ-साथ पिस्ज़कोवा को बेस्ट फैशन डिज़ाइनर अवार्ड - यूरोप से सम्मानित किया गया।

questions.question: Gagan Shakti 2024 exercise, recently conducted by Indian Air Force at which place?
questions.explanation: The Indian Air Force [IAF] is s conducted ‘Gagan Shakti-2024’, the nation’s largest air military exercise, involving all Air Force stations. Held every five years, it commenced on April 1, 2024, at Pokhran’s field firing range. > भारतीय वायु सेना [IAF] ने देश का सबसे बड़ा हवाई सैन्य अभ्यास ‘गगन शक्ति-2024’ आयोजित किया है, जिसमें सभी वायु सेना स्टेशन शामिल हैं। हर पाँच साल में आयोजित होने वाला यह अभ्यास 1 अप्रैल, 2024 को पोखरण के फील्ड फायरिंग रेंज में शुरू हुआ।

questions.question: Which article of the Constitution of India provisions for reservations of seats for scheduled castes and scheduled tribes in the House of People?
questions.explanation: Article 330 of the Indian Constitution has provisions for the Reservation of seats for Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes in the House of the People. > भारतीय संविधान के अनुच्छेद 330 में लोक सभा में अनुसूचित जातियों और अनुसूचित जनजातियों के लिए सीटों के आरक्षण का प्रावधान है।

questions.question: In whom are the executive power of the Indian Union and the supreme command of the defence forces of the Indian Union vested?
options: Chief of defence staff>चीफ ऑफ डिफेंस स्टाफ
questions.explanation: All the executive powers of the Union shall be vested in the President and he is the supreme commander of defence forces of the union. He makes the appointment of the chiefs of Army, Navy and the Air Force. > संघ की सभी कार्यकारी शक्तियाँ राष्ट्रपति में निहित होंगी और वह संघ के रक्षा बलों का सर्वोच्च कमांडर होगा। वह सेना, नौसेना और वायु सेना के प्रमुखों की नियुक्ति करता है।

questions.question: Majority of the fungal bionts of lichens belong to:
questions.explanation: Majority of the fungal bionts of lichens belong to class Ascomycetes or Basidiomycetes. Lichens are a complex life form that is a symbiotic partnership of two separate organisms, a fungus and an algal. > लाइकेन के अधिकांश फंगल जीव एस्कोमाइसीट्स या बेसिडियोमाइसीट्स वर्ग के हैं। लाइकेन एक जटिल जीवन रूप है जो दो अलग-अलग जीवों, एक कवक और एक शैवाल की सहजीवी साझेदारी है।

questions.question: Which type of cell is long and branched?
questions.explanation: Nerve cells, or neurons, are specialized cells of the nervous system that transmit electrical impulse. They are long and have branched extensions called dendrites and axons, which allow them to communicate with other nerve cells and carry signals over long distances. This unique structure allows for extensive networking between neurons. > तंत्रिका कोशिकाएँ या न्यूरॉन्स, तंत्रिका तंत्र की विशेष कोशिकाएँ हैं जो विद्युत आवेग संचारित करती हैं। वे लंबी होती हैं और उनमें डेंड्राइट्स और एक्सॉन नामक शाखाएँ होती हैं, जो उन्हें अन्य तंत्रिका कोशिकाओं के साथ संवाद करने और लंबी दूरी तक संकेतों को ले जाने की अनुमति देती हैं। यह अनूठी संरचना न्यूरॉन्स के बीच व्यापक नेटवर्किंग की अनुमति देती है।

questions.question: Which of the following statements about Duopoly is correct?
options: Duopoly is a special case for the Oligopoly market structure.>द्वयधिकार अल्पाधिकार बाजार संरचना के लिए एक विशेष मामला है।
Duopoly is a special case for the Monopsony market structure.>द्वयधिकार एकाधिकार बाजार संरचना के लिए एक विशेष मामला है।
In a duopoly, the actions of one firm do not affect the other. >द्वयधिकार में एक फर्म की गतिविधियों का दूसरे पर कोई प्रभाव नहीं पड़ता।
Duopoly is a special case for the Imperfect Competition.>द्वयधिकार अपूर्ण प्रतिस्पर्धा का एक विशेष मामला है।
questions.explanation: Duopoly is a special case of oligopoly. In a duopoly, two companies control the majority of a market, while in an oligopoly; a few companies control the market. > द्वयधिकार अल्पाधिकार का एक विशेष मामला है। द्वयाधिकार में, दो कंपनियाँ बाज़ार के अधिकांश भाग को नियंत्रित करती हैं, जबकि अल्पाधिकार में, कुछ कंपनियाँ बाज़ार को नियंत्रित करती हैं।

questions.question: What does Keynesian economics mainly focus on?
questions.explanation: Keynesian economics primarily focuses on national income and aggregate demand to understand and manage economic performance. It emphasizes how fluctuations in total spending [demand] can influence output and employment, especially in the short run. Company balance and resource allocation are not the central focus of Keynesian theory. > कीनेसियन अर्थशास्त्र मुख्य रूप से आर्थिक प्रदर्शन को समझने और प्रबंधित करने के लिए राष्ट्रीय आय और कुल मांग पर ध्यान केंद्रित करता है। यह इस बात पर जोर देता है कि कुल खर्च [मांग] में उतार-चढ़ाव किस तरह से उत्पादन और रोजगार को प्रभावित कर सकता है, खासकर अल्पावधि में। कंपनी संतुलन और संसाधन आवंटन कीनेसियन सिद्धांत का केंद्रीय फोकस नहीं है।

questions.question: Why is hydrogen chloride gas passed through a guard tube containing calcium chloride when it is prepared on a humid day?
options: Absorb the evolved gas>उत्सर्जित गैस को अवशोषित करना
Moisten the gas>गैस को नम करें
Absorb CI ions from the evolved gas >उत्सर्जित गैस से CI आयनों को अवशोषित करें
Absorb moisture from the gas>गैस से नमी को अवशोषित करना
questions.explanation: Hydrogen chloride gas is passed through a guard tube containing calcium chloride on a humid day because calcium chloride is a drying agent. It absorbs moisture from the air, preventing the hydrogen chloride gas from reacting with water vapor to form hydrochloric acid. This ensures that dry hydrogen chloride gas is collected. > हाइड्रोजन क्लोराइड गैस को नमी वाले दिन कैल्शियम क्लोराइड युक्त गार्ड ट्यूब से गुजारा जाता है क्योंकि कैल्शियम क्लोराइड एक सुखाने वाला एजेंट है। यह हवा से नमी को अवशोषित करता है, जिससे हाइड्रोजन क्लोराइड गैस को जल वाष्प के साथ प्रतिक्रिया करके हाइड्रोक्लोरिक एसिड बनाने से रोकता है। यह सुनिश्चित करता है कि सूखी हाइड्रोजन क्लोराइड गैस एकत्र की जाए।

questions.question: How many types of isotopes does Scandium have?
questions.explanation: Scandium is the element of the p-block of the periodic table, its atomic number is 21 and its atomic weight is 44.95u. It has 13 isotopes whose atomic weight varies from 40 to 52. > स्कैंडियम आवर्त सारणी के p-ब्लॉक का तत्व है, इसकी परमाणु संख्या 21 है और इसका परमाणु भार 44.95u है। इसके 13 समस्थानिक हैं जिनका परमाणु भार 40 से 52 तक होता है।

questions.question: What is the device used for converting AC into DC called?
questions.explanation: The device used for converting AC [alternating current] into DC [direct current] is called a rectifier. A rectifier works by allowing current to flow in only one direction. It uses components like diodes to block the negative half-cycles of AC, effectively converting the alternating current into a unidirectional [DC] current, which is suitable for powering electronic devices. > AC [प्रत्यावर्ती धारा] को DC [प्रत्यक्ष धारा] में बदलने के लिए इस्तेमाल किए जाने वाले उपकरण को रेक्टिफायर कहा जाता है। रेक्टिफायर करंट को केवल एक दिशा में प्रवाहित होने देकर काम करता है। यह AC के नकारात्मक आधे चक्रों को रोकने के लिए डायोड जैसे घटकों का उपयोग करता है, जो प्रभावी रूप से प्रत्यावर्ती धारा को एकतरफा [DC] करंट में परिवर्तित करता है, जो इलेक्ट्रॉनिक उपकरणों को बिजली देने के लिए उपयुक्त है।

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. He was _________ persisting with innocence.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is "Confidently." Explanation: The word "confidently" means acting with certainty, assurance, or self-belief, which fits the context of someone persisting with innocence, likely asserting their stance firmly. Here are the meanings and examples of all options: • Stridently - कर्कश रूप से – In a loud, forceful, or harsh manner. Example: "She stridently argued her point during the debate." • Harshly - कठोर रूप से – In a severe, rough, or unkind manner. Example: "The teacher harshly criticized his work." • Confidently - आत्मविश्वासपूर्वक – With certainty and assurance. Example: "He confidently defended his innocence in court." • Quickly - शीघ्रता से – At a fast pace. Example: "He quickly finished his homework." Conclusion: The best choice is "Confidently," as it correctly conveys the idea of persisting with innocence in a self-assured manner.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. Reaching late, the meeting had already begun.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: As I reached late Explanation: The original sentence "Reaching late, the meeting had already begun" is incorrect because it implies that the meeting had already begun due to reaching late, which doesn't make logical sense. The correct sentence should convey that the speaker reached late and, as a result, the meeting had already begun. • Option A "As I reached late" is the correct answer because it correctly uses the subordinating conjunction "as" to indicate the timing of the speaker's arrival. The other options are incorrect because: • Option B "Being late" is a gerund phrase that doesn't provide a clear connection to the rest of the sentence. • Option C "Having been late" is a present perfect participle phrase that implies the speaker's lateness had a prior effect, which isn't the intended meaning. Corrected Sentence: "As I reached late, the meeting had already begun."

questions.question: Select the Grammatically correct sentence -
options: Her separation is unbearable for me.
I should keep myself away from bad company.
He accidentally poisoned himself by eating wild mushrooms.
The two rivals never spoke to one another after the heated argument.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: He accidentally poisoned himself by eating wild mushrooms. Explanation: • Option [C] He accidentally poisoned himself by eating wild mushrooms. No grammatical error, as reflexive pronoun should be used after poison [when used as verb]. Why the other options are incorrect? • Option [A] Her separation is unbearable for me. Error: We do not use possessive adjective with separation. Corrected Sentence: Separation from her is unbearable for me. • Option [B] I should keep myself away from bad company. Error: Keep, stop, turn, hide do not take a reflexive pronoun after them. • Corrected Sentence: I should keep away from bad company. • Option [D] The two rivals never spoke to one another after the heated argument. Error: "One another" is used for more than two people, while "each other" is used for two. Corrected Sentence: The two rivals never spoke to each another after the heated argument.

questions.question: Identify the word that is correctly spelt.
questions.explanation: The correct spelling is "Substantially." Here are the correct spellings and meanings of all options: • Sentimentaly [Incorrect] → Sentimentally [Correct] – भावनात्मक रूप से – In a way that expresses emotions, especially tender feelings. Example: "She spoke sentimentally about her childhood memories." • Subsequentlly [Incorrect] → Subsequently [Correct] – बाद में / उसके बाद – After a particular thing has happened. Example: "He completed his degree and subsequently got a job." • Substantially [Correct] – काफी मात्रा में / मुख्य रूप से – To a great extent or largely. Example: "The company’s profits increased substantially this year." • Incidentaly [Incorrect] → Incidentally [Correct] – वैसे ही / संयोग से – By the way; used to introduce an additional remark. Example: "Incidentally, I saw your brother at the mall yesterday." Conclusion: The correctly spelled word is "Substantially."

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. The final event did not turn over the way everyone had anticipated, leading to unexpected results.
questions.explanation: Use "Turn out." Explanation: • The phrase "turn out" means to happen or develop in a particular way, which fits the context of the sentence. • The sentence conveys that the final event did not happen as expected, so "turn out" is the correct phrase. Meanings and Examples of All Options: • Turn up – आना / प्रकट होना – To arrive or appear unexpectedly. Example: "Many guests turned up late for the party." • Turn out – परिणामस्वरूप होना / उपस्थित होना – To happen in a certain way or to attend an event. Example: "The event turned out to be a grand success." • Turn on – चालू करना / उत्तेजित करना – To activate or excite. Example: "He turned on the TV to watch the news." • Turn over – उलटना / सौंपना – To flip something or transfer responsibility. Example: "She turned over the keys to the new owner." Conclusion: The final event did not Turn out the way everyone had anticipated, leading to unexpected results.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word in the following sentence - A plebiscite was held to decide the fate of the country.
questions.explanation: Sol: Correct Answer: Referendum Explanation: The word "plebiscite" refers to a direct vote by the people on an important public issue, which is synonymous with "referendum." In the given sentence, "A plebiscite was held to decide the fate of the country" means that the people voted directly on a crucial national decision. Let's see the meaning of all the words along with their usage: • Plebiscite – जनमत संग्रह – A direct vote in which the entire electorate is invited to accept or reject a proposal. Example: "The government held a plebiscite to determine the country’s independence." • Referendum – जनमत संग्रह – A general vote by the public on a single political question. Example: "The citizens participated in a referendum to decide on constitutional changes." • Evacuation – निकासी / खाली करना – The act of moving people from a place of danger. Example: "The evacuation of the city was necessary due to the approaching hurricane." • Probe – जांच / पड़ताल – A thorough investigation into a matter. Example: "The police launched a probe into the financial fraud." • Regime – शासन / सरकार – A system of government or rule. Example: "The new regime introduced several economic reforms." Conclusion: The correct synonym for "plebiscite" is "referendum", as both words refer to a public vote on an important issue.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. COPIOUS
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: Scarce Explanation: The word "copious" means abundant, plentiful, or in large quantities. The opposite of this would be something limited, insufficient, or lacking, which matches "scarce." Let's see the meaning of all the words along with their usage: • Copious – प्रचुर / बहुतायत में – In large amounts; plentiful. Example: "She took copious notes during the lecture." • Scarce – दुर्लभ / अल्प मात्रा में – In short supply; insufficient. Example: "Water is scarce in the desert regions." • Cleanse – साफ करना / शुद्ध करना – To clean or purify something. Example: "She used a special soap to cleanse her skin." • Profuse – अत्यधिक / बहुतायत में – Similar to "copious," meaning excessive or abundant. Example: "He offered profuse apologies for his mistake." • Hateful – घृणास्पद / नफरत से भरा – Full of hate or dislike. Example: "His hateful words hurt everyone in the room." Conclusion: The correct antonym for "copious" is "scarce," as copious refers to abundance, while scarce means a lack or shortage of something.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to make the sentence sensibly correct. The lawyer argued that the new law was not applicable ________ cases filed before its enactment.
questions.explanation: Explanation: The correct preposition to use with "applicable" when referring to something that is relevant to a particular case is "to." The phrase "applicable to" is the correct collocation, meaning that the law is relevant or pertains to certain cases. Corrected Sentence: "The lawyer argued that the new law was not applicable TO cases filed before its enactment."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning for the given idiom. Look before you leap
options: To rush into a decision without thinking
To take it easy and relax
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Think carefully before taking action. Explanation: "Look before you leap" – "कदम उठाने से पहले सोचें" This idiom means that one should carefully consider the consequences before making a decision or taking action. It advises caution to avoid mistakes or regrets. Origin of the Idiom: The phrase comes from an old fable where a careless animal jumps without checking the ground and falls into trouble. It metaphorically suggests that hasty actions can lead to negative consequences. Example: "Before investing all his savings in the new business, Rahul decided to look before he leapt." Hindi: "अपनी सारी बचत नए व्यवसाय में लगाने से पहले, राहुल ने सोच-समझकर फैसला लिया।" Conclusion: The correct meaning of "Look before you leap" is "Think carefully before taking action."

questions.question: Identify the segment in the sentence that contains grammatical error.
options: many changes in the rural area when
questions.explanation: The error is in segment: he last visited the countryside. CONCEPT: • The sentence is in the present perfect tense ["has observed"], but "last visited" is in the simple past tense. • The present perfect tense cannot be used with a specific past time reference like "last visited." • To maintain consistency, "he last visited" should be changed to "he has last visited" or rewritten using the simple past tense throughout. Corrected Sentence: "The Prime Minister observed many changes in the rural area when he last visited the countryside."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate SYNONYM of the given word. CLOISTERED
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Isolated. Let's see the meaning of all the words along with their usage: • Cloistered – एकांत में रहने वाला – Refers to being shut away from the outside world, secluded, or isolated. It often describes a lifestyle of solitude, like that of monks or nuns in a cloister. Example: "The cloistered monks rarely interacted with outsiders." • Isolated – अलग-थलग / एकाकी – Separated from others, lonely, or remote. Example: "The house was in an isolated location, far from the city." • Healing – चिकित्सा / उपचार – The process of recovering from an illness or injury. Example: "The doctor focused on healing his patients." • Confusion – उलझन / भ्रम – A state of being unclear or uncertain. Example: "There was confusion about the new rules." • Feeble – कमज़ोर / दुर्बल – Lacking strength or energy. Example: "The old man’s voice was feeble after the long illness." Conclusion: The correct synonym for "cloistered" is "isolated," as both words mean being secluded or separated from others.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the underlined word in the following sentence- I tried to PLACATE her by offering to pay for the repairs.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Enrage. Explanation: The word "placate" means to calm, pacify, or soothe someone’s anger or distress. The antonym should express the opposite, meaning to provoke or make someone angry. Let's see the meaning of all the words along with their usage: • Placate – शांत करना / तुष्ट करना – To make someone less angry or upset. Example: "She tried to placate the angry customer by offering a discount." • Enrage – क्रोधित करना / भड़काना – To make someone extremely angry. Example: "His rude remarks enrage his colleagues." • Please – खुश करना / संतुष्ट करना – To make someone happy or satisfied. Example: "She always tries to please her parents with good grades." • Ignore – अनदेखा करना / उपेक्षा करना – To deliberately not pay attention to something. Example: "He ignored the warning signs." • Notice – ध्यान देना / देखना – To observe or become aware of something. Example: "He noticed a mistake in the report." Conclusion: The correct antonym for "placate" is "enrage," as placate means to calm, while enrage means to provoke anger.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blanks. They must _______ all the enemy troops out of the city.
questions.explanation: The sentence should read: "They must rout all the enemy troops out of the city." This means that they must defeat and drive out the enemy forces completely. Explanation of the Other Options: • Root – "जड़ / उखाड़ना" – Refers to the underground part of a plant or the act of digging something out. questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words. A young child just beginning to walk
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Toddler. Here are the explanations for all options: • Infant – "शिशु" – A baby, typically under one year old, who is not yet able to walk. Example: "The infant slept peacefully in the crib." • Toddler – "नन्हा बच्चा" – A young child, typically between 1-3 years old, who has just started walking. Example: "The toddler took small, wobbly steps across the room." • Adolescent – "किशोर" – A young person going through puberty, generally between 13-19 years old. Example: "The adolescent struggled with teenage emotions and responsibilities." • Tween – "किशोरावस्था से पहले का बच्चा" – A child between 9-12 years old, before reaching full adolescence. Example: "As a tween, she was too old for toys but too young for teenage concerns." Conclusion: The correct choice is "Toddler," as it refers to a young child who has just started walking.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. The moment she left, the rest of the family started to pick her to pieces.
options: To criticize or analyse something severely
questions.explanation: Pick to pieces – आलोचना करना या गहराई से विश्लेषण करना "Pick to pieces" means to criticize or analyze something severely, often in a negative manner. It is used when someone dissects every flaw in a person, idea, or situation. Origin of this Idiom: The phrase originates from the idea of tearing something apart to inspect or criticize it thoroughly. It implies breaking down someone’s words or actions to find faults. Example: "As soon as the candidate finished his speech, the journalists picked his arguments to pieces." [This means the journalists severely analyzed and criticized his arguments.] Hindi Translation: "जैसे ही उम्मीदवार ने अपना भाषण समाप्त किया, पत्रकारों ने उसकी दलीलों की कड़ी आलोचना की।" Conclusion: The correct answer is "To criticize or analyse something severely," as it best represents the meaning of "Pick to pieces."

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words given. Science regarding principles of classification
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Taxonomy. Meanings and Examples of the Given Options: • Autonomy – "स्वायत्तता / आत्मनिर्भरता" – The ability to govern oneself or function independently. Example: "The new law granted more autonomy to local governments." • Ontology – "अस्तित्व विज्ञान" – A branch of philosophy that deals with the nature of being and existence. Example: "Ontology explores questions like 'What is the meaning of existence?'" • Meteorology – "मौसम विज्ञान" – The scientific study of weather and atmospheric conditions. Example: "Meteorology helps predict storms and climate patterns." • Taxonomy – "वर्गीकरण विज्ञान" – The scientific system of classification, especially of organisms. Example: "Linnaeus is known as the father of taxonomy for his work in classifying species." Conclusion: The correct word for "Science regarding principles of classification" is "Taxonomy," as it refers to the systematic classification of organisms, concepts, or things.

questions.question: Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful paragraph. A. The ecosystem is vital for human survival. B. It supports a variety of plant and animal life. C. Ecosystem conservation is crucial for maintaining biodiversity. D. Human activities are increasingly threatening ecosystems worldwide.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: DCAB This order makes sense because: • Sentence D introduces the issue: human activities threatening ecosystems. • Sentence C emphasizes the importance of conservation. • Sentence A highlights the ecosystem's importance for humans. • Sentence B elaborates on the ecosystem's role in supporting life. Final paragraph: "Human activities are increasingly threatening ecosystems worldwide. Ecosystem conservation is crucial for maintaining biodiversity. The ecosystem is vital for human survival. It supports a variety of plant and animal life."

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to change the voice of the given sentence. They ought to follow the rules strictly.
options: The rules ought to be followed strictly.
The rules ought to be follow strictly.
The rules ought to being followed strictly.
The rules ought to have been followed strictly.
questions.explanation: Explanation: Active voice structure: They [Subject] + ought to follow [Verb] + the rules [Object] + strictly. Passive voice structure: The rules [Object] + ought to be followed [Verb3] + strictly. • This option correctly changes the sentence from active to passive voice, making "the rules" the focus of the sentence instead of "they." • The phrase "ought to follow" is converted into "ought to be followed" [past participle form] to maintain the correct passive structure. • "Strictly" remains in the same position, as adverbs generally do not change in passive transformations. Corrected Sentence: "The rules ought to be followed strictly."

questions.question: Change the narration of the given sentence. She told me that she had never seen such a beautiful sunset before.
options: She told me, "I have never see such a beautiful sunset before."
She told me, "I had never seen such a beautiful sunset before."
She told me, "I never see such a beautiful sunset before."
She told me, "I will never see such a beautiful sunset before."
questions.explanation: Explanation: • The given sentence is in indirect speech and uses past perfect tense ["had never seen"]. • To convert it to direct speech, we replace "she" with "I" and remove "that" while keeping "had never seen" unchanged. • The phrase "before" remains the same, as it does not affect the tense transformation. Corrected Sentence: She told me, ‘'I had never seen such a beautiful sunset before.’’
